name: 12 Apostolov
description: A ship of the line of the Black Sea Fleet, it participated in the defense of Sevastopol during the Crimean War. It was scuttled along with other ships on February 13, 1855, to block Anglo-French ships from entering the harbor. Fortunately, the Archipelago's shipwrights managed to recover its blueprints.

name: De Zeven Provincien
description: The flagship of Dutch Admiral de Ruyter, launched in 1665. A veteran of many victorious naval battles, the ship was named in honor of the seven autonomous provinces that formed the Dutch Republic in the 17th century.

name: Huracan
description: Named after the Mayan god of wind - Huracan, this giant is a symbol of the Inhagger Empire's military might, a native civilization whose technological level rivals and often surpasses that of the Archipelago states.

name: La Couronne
description: The first ship of the line in the French Navy, "La Couronne" was laid down at the La Roche Bernard shipyard in 1629 at the personal initiative of Cardinal Richelieu. It served as the flagship during the Battle of Guetaria, where the new French fleet defeated a Spanish squadron.

name: La Royale
description: A French galleass from the late 17th century. Based on the traditional Mediterranean galley, but built on a larger scale.

name: Santisima Trinidad
description: The largest Spanish ship of the line, which fought at the Battle of Trafalgar. While heavy and unwieldy, she could easily devastate both land fortifications and entire squadrons of enemy frigates.

name: Sovereign
description: A British ship of the line which served as inspiration for many of the largest warships during the 17th century. Considered to be the most powerful ship during its time, she was heavily armed but suffered from poor stability due to its weight.

name: Victory
description: The flagship of Horatio Nelson, one of the greatest and largest warships of the British Royal Navy, immortalized at the Battle of Trafalgar. Launched in 1765, she remains the oldest commissioned warship in the world with 245 years of service as of 2023.

name: Adventure
description: The oared frigate of William Kidd, the pirate hunter who became a pirate himself. First launched in 1695, it sailed thousands of miles across the Atlantic and Indian Oceans.

name: Firestorm
description: With a fearsome appearance, powerful armament, a strong hull, and a large cargo hold, the "Firestorm" became a legendary Imperial ship. The first "Firestorms" were captured and studied after Longhorn's victory over the Immortal Admirals.

name: Ingermanland
description: A warship of the Baltic Fleet of the Russian Empire, designed by Peter the Great. It was the first of its class and one of the Tsar's favorite ships. During the Great Northern War, it served as the flagship of both the Russian and the combined Russo-Danish-Dutch-English fleets, occasionally sailing under Peter's personal standard.

name: La Sirene
description: A French ship of the line from the late 17th century. Launched in 1691, she participated in the War of the League of Augsburg. During the War of Spanish Succession (1701-1713), she was captured by the English and destroyed in Vigo Bay in 1702.

name: Montanes
description: This ship was funded by the people of Cantabria ("the mountain" region of Spain, hence the name Montañés - "the Highlander"). It became the benchmark for later Spanish 74-gun ships of the line of the late 18th century. It took part in almost every major naval engagement from the late 18th to the early 19th century.

name: Neptuno
description: Originally a 17th-century Spanish Manila galleon, heavily armed and used to transport Spanish treasures from the Philippines to Mexico. After arriving in the Archipelago, the ship was redesigned and earned widespread fame.

name: Octopus
description: A warship built by the Empire's shipwrights. It was first captured during a battle with the Cult of the Witnesses of the Apocalypse. According to the dying captain of the cultists, these ships were found drifting crewless somewhere in the Endless Sea.

name: Redoutable
description: A famed French ship of the line that duelled with HMS Victory during the Battle of Trafalgar. The fatal shot killing Vice-Admiral Horatio Nelson was fired from this very ship.

name: Sans Pareil
description: Originally a French ship of the line, she was captured by Englishmen during the "Glorious First of June" battle in 1794. She served actively in the Royal Navy until 1807, participating in several engagements and capturing enemy vessels. In 1807, she was converted into a prison ship.

name: St. Pavel
description: A distinguished ship-of-the-line of the Black Sea Fleet that has been through many battles. From 1784 to 1788, her captain was Fyodor Ushakov, the future legendary Russian admiral.

name: Vasa
description: A ship meant to be the pride of the Swedish Empire but became known for sinking on its maiden voyage. Severian shipbuilders fixed its design flaws and built a copy destined for glory in battle.

name: Anson
description: A warship with an impressive service record. She fought at the Battle of the Saintes on April 9, 1782, under Admiral Sir George Rodney against Admiral de Grasse. In 1794, she was rebuilt as a large 44-gun frigate and later took part in the Revolutionary and Napoleonic wars.

name: Azov
description: Flagship of the Black Sea Fleet, hero of the Battle of Navarino. The first Russian ship awarded the St. George stern flag for victory over five Turkish ships.

name: Bellona
description: The first ship of the line approved after Lord Anson became First Lord of the Admiralty. She took part in the Seven Years' War, the American War of Independence, and the Napoleonic Wars.

name: Deadfish
description: These Imperial ships were first deployed at the Battle of Devios as part of the "Iron Squadron." Slow, heavily armored, and equipped with powerful weaponry, they withstood the brunt of the Espanyola fleet. The battle site became known as Lagrimas de Viudas - Widows' Tears Sea.

name: Iberia
description: One of the earliest ships of the Española school of shipbuilding. This frigate class was first laid down at the San Martinas shipyard. Widely employed for patrol duty, protecting trade routes from pirates, and conducting raids deep into enemy waters.

name: Kobukson
description: The "turtle ship" served as the core of the Korean fleet during the 16th and 17th centuries. In addition to her unique covered design, she wields combined armament, including mortars.

name: Le Saint Louis
description: Line ship commissioned by Cardinal Richelieu as part of the naval reinforcement program. Participated in several battles and ultimately burned during the Battle of La Hougue after being attacked by English fireships.

name: Mordaunt
description: Constructed in 1681 with private funds by Charles Mordaunt, Earl of Peterborough, and named after him. The ship was too heavily armed for a private vessel, leading to its confiscation in 1683. After legal battles, it was later purchased by the Royal Navy.

name: Poltava
description: Named after the victory over the Swedish Empire at the Battle of Poltava, she was the first ship of the line to be laid down and built at the Saint Petersburg Admiralty. She features a "Dutch" transom and sculptures of Saint George as its decoration.

name: Prins Willem
description: A 17th-century East Indiaman, belonging to the Dutch East India Company and the largest ship in their fleet. Her maiden voyage to the Dutch East Indies was in 1651. After several voyages, she sank near Madagascar in 1662. Fortunately, her blueprints are still in the hands of local shipwrights.

name: Shen
description: Main combat ship of the Kai fleet. Has a fearsome appearance, sturdy hull, hybrid armament and mortars, allowing it to fight in any conditions

name: Constitution
description: One of the original six frigates of the United States Navy, the "Constitution" was larger and more heavily armed than its contemporaries. She gained fame during the War of 1812 against Great Britain, earning the nickname "Old Ironsides" and the adoration of many.

name: Devourer
description: The "Devourer" is an Imperial ship with heavy armament on both the bow and stern. Its thick hull offers excellent protection, while jibs ensure good maneuverability.

name: Essex
description: A heavily-armed American frigate. It served in the First Barbary War and the War of 1812, where it captured 13 whaling ships during a single cruise. Later captured by the British, the "Essex" went on to serve under the British Crown.

name: Falmouth
description: Originally designed for trade expeditions and provisioning cities with its massive hold, this ship can also hold its own in combat. It gained an unfortunate reputation as a slave transporter during the height of the Atlantic slave trade.

name: Flying Cloud
description: Clippers are vessels with the most powerful sail rig and a unique hull design that minimized water resistance at the cost of maneuverability. This ship held the record for the fastest passage between New York and San Francisco - 89 days and 8 hours.

name: Friedrich Wilhelm
description: German "great" frigate of the 17th century. On July 25, 1692, the Friedrich Wilhelm departed Emden for Guinea. During the voyage, it defeated a French vessel.

name: Red Arrow
description: First built by Maurice "Shipwright" Fleury in the port of Aruba. After falling victim to a plot by the Levenhook brothers, Maurice was tragically executed. In his memory, a skeleton holding a chalice - a symbol of grief - and a spear - a symbol of revenge - adorns the bow.

name: Sparrow
description: Tartanes were widely used across the Mediterranean - from Turkey to Spain - for a variety of tasks. They were especially favored by Algerian corsairs. Upon reaching the Archipelago, the tartane became popular with local pirates, particularly after Tortuga craftsmen fitted it with mortars.

name: Surprise
description: The French ship Unite was captured and commissioned into the Royal Navy as HMS Surprise. She was considered a hybrid between a corvette and a frigate. The Surprise became famous in 1799 when she recaptured HMS Hermione from the French.

name: Three Hierarchs
description: This ship belonged to the large and successful "Slava Rossii" class of ships of the line (built from 1733 to 1774 in St. Petersburg and Arkhangelsk; 58 vessels in total), known for their seaworthiness, maneuverability, and stability. "Three Hierarchs" was one of the key ships of the first major ocean expedition of the Russian fleet from the Baltic to the Mediterranean, and a symbol of the Chesme victory.

name: Axel Thorsen
description: Norwegian gunboat schooner, one of ten built for the Dano-Norwegian fleet before the end of the Napoleonic Wars, when Norway became independent from Denmark under the Treaty of Kiel in 1814.

name: Black Prince
description: The combat galleon of the pirate prince Morgan Black, rumored to have been gifted to him by the Empire for his service. It's one of the best-armed ships of its class.

name: Black Wind
description: The "Black Wind" has a sturdy hull and can accommodate a large crew, providing solid protection in battle. However, this comes at the cost of a reduced cargo hold, making her less suitable for trade or prolonged engagements.

name: Eagle
description: Originally a Dutch fluyt, it underwent significant redesign after arriving at the Archipelago. Many mistake it for the lightly armed "Friede", only to realize their error too late as mortar shells tear through their decks.

name: Kwee Song
description: A Korean ship with a distinctive armored deck resembling a turtle shell, hence its name "turtle ship." The first of these ships fought the Japanese fleet at Sacheon. Local shipwrights further refined the design.

name: La Creole
description: A lightweight and fast corvette of the French Navy with formidable armament. It took part in the First Franco-Mexican War and the bombardment of Fort San Juan de Ulua near Veracruz.

name: Le Requin
description: French frigates and combat sloops were ineffective against the Berber pirates, who used the agile xebecs. Logically speaking, if they are able to make such good use of them, then why not try those xebecs ourselves? The original name, meaning "Shark" when translated from French, was launched in the year 1751 in Toulon, where it soon made the lives of those pirates more difficult.

name: Russia
description: A sail frigate of the Russian Empire, laid down in 1724 during the reign of Peter the Great by master shipbuilder Richard Browne. Launched in 1728, it participated in two wars, and in 1734, during the blockade of Danzig, it captured the French 30-gun frigate "Le Brilliant".

name: San Martin
description: The flagship of the Duke of Medina Sidonia, commander of the Spanish Armada. First launched in 1580, she played a crucial role in defending the Armada as she sailed through the English Channel. She survived the storm and English cannons to return safely to Spain.

name: Southampton
description: This frigate served the fleet for over 55 years, taking part both in naval operations and in delicate diplomatic missions, including transporting members of the royal family. The Southampton became the prototype for the 32-gun frigates of the same name, which played an important role in the British fleet in the late 18th century.

name: Balloon
description: A marvelous invention of 1783. You can control altitude, speed, and direction, but cannot move against the wind. The balloon can be attacked and risks crashing into mountain terrain.

name: Golden Apostle
description: Made from high-quality lightweight woods, this reconfigured combat schooner boasts excellent seafaring abilities and maneuverability. Pirates once mistook its flashy appearance for a sign of an inexperienced traveler - only to fall into an ambush themselves.

name: La Salamandre
description: Built in 1752 in Toulon, she was successfully used to bombard Berber pirate bases in Algeria.

name: Le Cerf
description: Launched on March 2, 1779, in Saint-Malo, the "Le Cerf" patrolled the coast of Brittany and fought its first battle against two English cutters on June 21. She became famous for her evasiveness.

name: Mercury
description: These clumsy but reliable ships sailed between the New and Old Worlds for over 200 years, transporting goods and treasures, including gold from South America to Spain. They became prime targets for pirates, leading to a long conflict between brigantines and galleons.

name: Phoenix
description: The first two of these ships were spotted near Grey Island, crewed by Russian sailors and officers who fled the Tsar's rule in search of freedom.

name: Polacca
description: The Venetian fleet kept armed polaccas in service to protect trade routes in the Adriatic Sea from Dalmatian, Greek, and Turkish pirates

name: Savannah
description: A paddle-wheeled steamboat, found frozen in the northern ice of the Archipelago. No survivors were found, leaving the mystery of how it ended up there. Despite its revolutionary design, the inefficient, massive steam engine requires large amounts of coal, leaving the ship poorly armed and unsuited to harsh conditions.

name: Shunsen
description: "Red Seal Ship". Such ships were used by the Tokugawa shogunate for trade and diplomatic missions to Southeast Asian ports. Upon reaching the Archipelago, it was converted into an excellent warship.

name: Friede
description: A revolutionary Dutch ship from the late 16th century, designed to optimize transoceanic voyages by maximizing hold capacity and crew efficiency. The fluyt gave Dutch traders a competitive edge, helping the Netherlands rise to naval power.

name: Horizont
description: The brigantine gained fame among pirates thanks to its seafaring capabilities, maneuverability, and speed. Even the name changed its meaning, literally becoming "brigantine" (in Italian, brigante means "outlaw, pirate")

name: Pickle
description: A classic topsail schooner with light armament. It became famous for delivering the news of Nelson's victory at the Battle of Trafalgar to England. Why is it that new captains arrive in the Archipelago aboard this schooner?...
